Core appraisal in a heat context: Heat or cooling is appraised as pleasant, welcome, or relieving; the situation is manageable or even desirable (treat, comfort, leisure).
Typical narrative role in heat perceptio: Everyday coping and relief; playful adaptation to heat; positive framing of cooling practices (e.g. treats, holidays, cosy warmth).
Illustrative cues: “love this weather”, “finally nice and warm”, “this AC is heaven”, “ice cream saves my life”

Core appraisal in a heat context: Heat conditions are appraised as unexpected, abnormal or record-breaking, relative to local or seasonal expectations.
Typical narrative role in heat perceptio: Monitoring and remarking on abnormality; emphasising records, anomalies or temporal shifts (“still this hot?”, “in this season?”).
Illustrative cues: “can’t believe it’s this hot”, “30°C in October??”, “still this warm at night”, “never seen heat like this”

Core appraisal in a heat context: Situation is appraised in a descriptive, informational way with minimal affect; focus is on reporting rather than evaluation.
Typical narrative role in heat perceptio: Observation, monitoring and reporting: forecasts, warnings, statistics, factual descriptions that provide context but little emotional stance.
Illustrative cues: “it’s 33°C today”, “heat warning in effect”, “forecast 35°C all week”, “UV index is 10”

Core appraisal in a heat context: Heat is appraised as burden, exhaustion or loss of ease/opportunity; people feel worn down, drained or constrained.
Typical narrative role in heat perceptio: Encoding perceived disruption of routines and wellbeing: sleep loss, lack of energy, curtailed activities, emotional fatigue.
Illustrative cues: “this heat is depressing”, “no energy to do anything”, “can’t face another hot night”, “summer is ruining my mood”

Core appraisal in a heat context: Heat is appraised as a threat to health, safety or systems, with anticipated harm to self, others or the climate.
Typical narrative role in heat perceptio: Risk alerting and anticipatory concern: health warnings, worry about vulnerable groups, linking heat to serious climate risks.
Illustrative cues: “worried about heatstroke”, “this heat is dangerous for the elderly”, “scared for people sleeping rough”, “terrifying what climate change is doing”

Core appraisal in a heat context: Heat-related conditions are appraised as unfair, avoidable or caused/worsened by someone’s decisions; perceived norm violation or injustice.
Typical narrative role in heat perceptio: Governance and justice framing; blaming landlords, authorities or systems for inadequate protection, infrastructure or response.
Illustrative cues: “can’t believe the landlord still won’t fix the AC”, “this city never plans for heatwaves”, “it’s outrageous the train has no AC in this heat”

Core appraisal in a heat context: Heat is appraised through revulsion, contamination or environmental spoilage, often bodily or sensory.
Typical narrative role in heat perceptio: Marking environmental degradation and bodily discomfort: sweaty, smelly, ‘sauna-like’ spaces, or dirty, stifling environments.
Illustrative cues: “so sweaty and gross”, “the bus smells disgusting in this heat”, “my apartment feels like a sticky sauna”, “it’s nasty out there”
